UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 613, JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: DON FEINBERG. PLAINTIFFS DESHAUN WILLIAMS, JAMES CAUSEY, IRENE BENTON, LEE VICTOR, IRMA CRANDLE AND ROBERT EASTVELDS' MOTION TO SERVER THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FROM THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SEVER 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FROM FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. SEVERANCE OF THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION WILL NOT ADVANCE ANY OF THE CCP 1048(B) GROUNDS AS ALLEGATIONS IN THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION ARE RELATED TO REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION IN FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. (FAC 75). SFRO 37.9(A)(11) AND (12) IS NOT MENTIONED IN THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 613, JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: DON FEINBERG (NOT REPORTED) DEFENDANTS SUKHDEVJI PATEL, SANGITA PATEL AND AMISH PATELS' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE CLASS ALLEGATIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND CONTINUED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION EXCEPT 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS SHALL FILE THEIR MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION AND SERVE BY PERSONAL SERVICE NO LATER THAN AUGUST 28, 2013 BY 4 P.M. TO BE SET FOR HEARING DURING THE WEEK OF SEPTEMBER 30, 2013. HEARING ON THE MOTION TO STRIKE CLASS ALLEGATIONS RE: 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS CONTINUED OCTOBER 9, 2013. IF PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO FILE AND SERVE THEIR MOTION BY AUGUST 28, 2013 BY 4 P.M., DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION. (D613)

ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS JAMES ELLIS, JAMES COX, ALDOMAR ALCARAZ, CARIDAD GAONZALES AND JESSE JOHNSON'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS SUKHDEVIJI PATEL AND SANGITA PATEL AND THEIR ATTORNEY OF RECORD - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED. IN THIS CASE CENTERING ON HABITABILITY ISSUES IN A SINGLE ROOM OCCUPANCY HOTEL, BOTH DEFENDANTS HAVE PROPOUNDED IDENTICAL SETS OF 228 SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES TO EACH PLAINTIFF, FOR A TOTAL OF 1,824 SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES. DEFENDANTS HAVE FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE JUSTIFICATION FOR EXCEEDING THE 35 INTERROGATORY LIMITATION OF CCP 2030.040. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PRO TEM JUDGE: ERNEST HALPERIN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion For A Protective Order; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defts Sukhdeviji Patel And Sangita Patel And Their Attorney Of Record; Declaration
Off Calendar Aug-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHRISTOPHER R. BOLDIG, TEL. NO. 318-5709 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS JAMES ELLIS, JAMES COX, ALDOMAR ALCARAZ, CARIDAD GAONZALES AND JESSE JOHNSON'S Motion For A Protective Order; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants Sukhdeviji Patel And Sangita Patel And Their Attorney Of Record - Plaintiff's motion for a protective order is GRANTED. In this case centering on habitability issues in a single room occupancy hotel, both defendants have propounded identical sets of 228 specially prepared interrogatories to each plaintiff, for a total of 1,824 specially prepared interrogatories. Defendants have failed to demonstrate justification for exceeding the 35 interrogatory limitation of CCP 2030.040. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Pro Tem Judge: Ernest Halperin, Not reported.
=(302/JPT)
